title: Apply plant biology to agronomic practices
training_package_name: Agriculture, Horticulture and Conservation and Land Management Training Package
competent_person_statement: A competent person in Apply plant biology to agronomic practices demonstrates consistent performance across all elements and performance criteria in a workplace context.

elements.title: Apply plant taxonomy to agronomic practices

pcs.text: Identify botanical terminology of plant taxonomy, including plant kingdom divisions, major plant families and genera for plant classification
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Identify botanical terminology of plant taxonomy, including plant kingdom divisions, major plant families and genera for plant classification?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Identify botanical terminology of plant taxonomy, including plant kingdom divisions, major plant families and genera for plant classification.

pcs.text: Apply the rules of plant nomenclature when naming plants
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Apply the rules of plant nomenclature when naming plants?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Apply the rules of plant nomenclature when naming plants.

pcs.text: Identify the external features of plants, including leaves, stems, flowers and fruits
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Identify the external features of plants, including leaves, stems, flowers and fruits?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Identify the external features of plants, including leaves, stems, flowers and fruits.

pcs.text: Identify a range of plants used in agronomy to species level, using plant botanical key and other references where required
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Identify a range of plants used in agronomy to species level, using plant botanical key and other references where required?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Identify a range of plants used in agronomy to species level, using plant botanical key and other references where required.

pcs.text: Use botanical terminology to discuss plant taxonomy in agronomic practices with appropriate personnel
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Use botanical terminology to discuss plant taxonomy in agronomic practices with appropriate personnel?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Use botanical terminology to discuss plant taxonomy in agronomic practices with appropriate personnel.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E1: Apply plant taxonomy to agronomic practices

elements.title: Identify plant functions and their impact on growth

pcs.text: Investigate and identify plant cell structures, their functions and the organisation of cells into primary tissues
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Investigate and identify plant cell structures, their functions and the organisation of cells into primary tissues?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Investigate and identify plant cell structures, their functions and the organisation of cells into primary tissues.

pcs.text: Research the structure and functions of leaves, stems, root and flowers in relation to agronomic practices
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Research the structure and functions of leaves, stems, root and flowers in relation to agronomic practices?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Research the structure and functions of leaves, stems, root and flowers in relation to agronomic practices.

pcs.text: Identify the processes and outcomes of photosynthesis, respiration and transpiration

pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Identify the processes and outcomes of photosynthesis, respiration and transpiration.

elements.analysis_focus: Analyse evidence against Element E2: Identify plant functions and their impact on growth

elements.title: Apply knowledge of plant morphology to agronomic practices

pcs.text: Research, analyse and document specialist botanical knowledge of plant morphology, including leaf, root, stem, flower and seed characteristics from development to maturity for crop and pasture management
pcs.analysis_prompt: What valid, sufficient, authentic and current evidence shows the candidate can Research, analyse and document specialist botanical knowledge of plant morphology, including leaf, root, stem, flower and seed characteristics from development to maturity for crop and pasture management?
pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Research, analyse and document specialist botanical knowledge of plant morphology, including leaf, root, stem, flower and seed characteristics from development to maturity for crop and pasture management.

pcs.text: Use botanical terminology when discussing plant morphology and growth stages

pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Use botanical terminology when discussing plant morphology and growth stages.

pcs.text: Identify critical growth stages for crop and pasture monitoring, nutrient assessment and spray applications

pcs.benchmark_statement: The candidate demonstrates that they can Identify critical growth stages for crop and pasture monitoring, nutrient assessment and spray applications.
